paper.title: High Velocity Stars in SDSS/APOGEE DR17

review.summary: This paper identifies 23 high-velocity stars (v_GC > 450 km/s) from APOGEE DR17 complemented with Gaia eDR3 astrometry and Bailer-Jones 2021 distances. The data assets include four main data tables: (1) astrometric parameters (coordinates, parallaxes, distances); (2) kinematic parameters (proper motions, radial velocities, Galactocentric velocities); (3) orbital parameters in the Irrgang Model I potential; (4) stellar parameters (magnitudes, T_eff, [Fe/H], [alpha/Fe]). An additional table compares isochrone-derived and StarHorse2 masses/radii. A comparison table contrasts Aoki et al. (2007) and APOGEE DR17 measurements for the peculiar CEMP star 2M22242563-0438021. A parameter table defines the Irrgang Model I gravitational potential. External software resources with explicit URLs include the Doppler RV pipeline, the FERRE code, and the gaiadr3_zeropoint parallax correction package. No paper-specific data repository URL is provided.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents equatorial coordinates, Gaia eDR3 parallaxes, and Bailer-Jones 2021 photogeometric distances for the 23 high-velocity star candidates. The last three rows list the three stars exceeding the escape velocity in the MWPotential2014 model (unbound candidates).

source_refs.caption: Equatorial coordinates, paralaxes from Gaia eDR3, and distances from \citet{BailerJones2021} for the HiVel stars. The last three rows list the stars that exceed the escape velocity in the MWPotential2014 model (unbound candidates; Sect. \ref{subsec:unb_cand}).
source_refs.label: tab:hvs_astrometric_parameter1

columns.name: \varpi | (mas)
columns.meaning: Parallax from Gaia eDR3

columns.name: d_\mathrmBJ | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Photogeometric distance from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021)

internal_tables.evidence: Equatorial coordinates, paralaxes from Gaia eDR3, and distances from \citet{BailerJones2021} for the HiVel stars. The last three rows list the stars that exceed the escape velocity in the MWPotential2014 model (unbound candidates; Sect. \ref{subsec:unb_cand}).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents proper motions from Gaia eDR3, radial velocities from APOGEE DR17 (and Gaia eDR3 when available), and derived Galactocentric velocities for the 23 high-velocity stars. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

source_refs.caption: Proper motions from Gaia eDR3, radial velocities from APOGEE DR17, radial velocity from Gaia eDR3 (when available), and estimated Galactocentric velocity for the HiVel stars. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents orbital parameters derived for the 23 high-velocity stars using the Irrgang Model I gravitational potential. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

source_refs.caption: Orbital parameters derived for the HiVel stars in the Irrgang Model I potential: perigalacticon and apogalacticon, orbital eccentricity, maximum height over the Galactic plane, and orbital energy. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents observed stellar properties for the 23 high-velocity stars: absolute magnitudes from Gaia eDR3, and spectroscopic parameters (effective temperature, metallicity, alpha-element abundance) from APOGEE DR17. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

source_refs.caption: Observed properties of the HiVel stars: absolute magnitudes from Gaia eDR3, effective temperature, metalicity and $\alpha$-elements abundance from APOGEE DR17. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.
source_refs.label: tab:Stellar_parameters

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compares surface gravity, mass, and radius estimates derived from APOGEE DR17 stellar parameters combined with MIST isochrones against independent determinations from the StarHorse2 catalog and Gaia eDR3 for the 23 high-velocity stars. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

source_refs.caption: Comparison of the $\log g$ values from APOGEE DR17 and the corresponding isochrone-derived masses and radii, with the values provided by the StarHorse2 catalog \citep{Anders2022}. The StarHorse2 radius has been derived from the corresponding $\log g$ and $M$ values. The two stars that do not have entries in the StarHorse2 catalog, have mass estimates in \citet{Anders2019} -- see text for details. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.
source_refs.label: tab:stellar_masses

internal_tables.evidence: Comparison of the $\log g$ values from APOGEE DR17 and the corresponding isochrone-derived masses and radii, with the values provided by the StarHorse2 catalog \citep{Anders2022}. The StarHorse2 radius has been derived from the corresponding $\log g$ and $M$ values. The two stars that do not have entries in the StarHorse2 catalog, have mass estimates in \citet{Anders2019} -- see text for details. The last three rows correspond to the unbound candidates.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compares stellar parameters, chemical abundances [X/Fe], and radial velocity for the CEMP star 2M22242563-0438021 (HE 2221-0453) between literature values from Aoki et al. (2007) and APOGEE DR17 automatic pipeline measurements. All abundances are in dex.

source_refs.caption: Comparison of stellar parameters, chemical abundances [X/Fe], and radial velocities of the star 2M22242563-0438021 (HE 2221-0453), as given by \citet{Aoki2007} and by APOGEE DR17. All abundances are in dex.
source_refs.label: tab:cemp_properties

internal_tables.evidence: Comparison of stellar parameters, chemical abundances [X/Fe], and radial velocities of the star 2M22242563-0438021 (HE 2221-0453), as given by \citet{Aoki2007} and by APOGEE DR17. All abundances are in dex.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the best-fit parameters of the Irrgang Model I gravitational potential used for orbital integration of the high-velocity stars.

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the gravitational potential parameter (bulge, disk, or halo component)

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Best-fit value with uncertainty for the parameter

external_resources.description: GitHub repository for the Doppler radial velocity determination algorithm, used in the updated APOGEE DR17 data reduction pipeline.

source_refs.context:
Data products from spectra taken in both APOGEE surveys are included in SDSS DR17 \citep{Abdurrouf2022}, after being automatically recalculated \citep{Nidever2015,Holtzman2015} using the latest APOGEE data reduction pipeline,
%FVR: sorry but I was unable to find this reference \citep{Holtzman2022}
which uses an updated algorithm in DR17, {\texttt{Doppler}}\footnote{\url{https://github.com/dnidever/doppler}}, for radial velocity determination; this algorithm has improved the derivation of radial velocities for fainter sources.  
The APOGEE Stellar Parameters and Chemical Abundances Pipeline \citep[\texttt{ASPCAP};][]{GarciaPerez2016}, which is rooted in the {\texttt{FERRE}}\footnote{\url{https://github.com/callendeprieto/ferre}} code of \citet{AllendePrieto2006}, provides atmospheric parameters ($T_\mathrm{eff}$, $\log g$, [Fe/H]) and chemical abundances measurements for up to 20 chemical species for 733\,901 sources.
\texttt{ASPCAP} estimates the stellar atmospheric parameters by comparing observed spectra against a

external_resources.evidence: Referenced in a footnote as the software implementing the updated radial velocity algorithm for fainter sources in APOGEE DR17.

external_resources.description: GitHub repository for the FERRE code, the spectral synthesis engine underlying the APOGEE ASPCAP pipeline for stellar parameter and abundance estimation.

source_refs.context:
%FVR: sorry but I was unable to find this reference \citep{Holtzman2022}
which uses an updated algorithm in DR17, {\texttt{Doppler}}\footnote{\url{https://github.com/dnidever/doppler}}, for radial velocity determination; this algorithm has improved the derivation of radial velocities for fainter sources.  
The APOGEE Stellar Parameters and Chemical Abundances Pipeline \citep[\texttt{ASPCAP};][]{GarciaPerez2016}, which is rooted in the {\texttt{FERRE}}\footnote{\url{https://github.com/callendeprieto/ferre}} code of \citet{AllendePrieto2006}, provides atmospheric parameters ($T_\mathrm{eff}$, $\log g$, [Fe/H]) and chemical abundances measurements for up to 20 chemical species for 733\,901 sources.
\texttt{ASPCAP} estimates the stellar atmospheric parameters by comparing observed spectra against a 
library of MARCS stellar atmospheres \citep{Meszaros2012,Jonsson2020},

external_resources.evidence: Referenced in a footnote as the code base for ASPCAP, which derives atmospheric parameters and chemical abundances for APOGEE sources.

external_resources.description: GitLab repository for the gaiadr3_zeropoint Python package used to correct Gaia eDR3 parallaxes for zero-point bias before stellar property inference.
external_resources.evidence: Referenced in a footnote as the Python package used to apply zero-point bias corrections to Gaia eDR3 parallaxes for the mass and radius analysis.
